title: Packet 095 reports impossible execution results and cites missing cross-runtime transcripts
claim: Packet 095 cannot support its claimed 18/18 PASS verdict because the summary simultaneously claims 15 PASS/3 SKIP and 18 PASS/0 SKIP, while CR-016 and CR-018 transcripts are missing at the cited path pattern.
counterevidenceSought: Corrected table proving 15 PASS/3 SKIP was intended
Non-/tmp evidence ledger mapping CR-016/017/018 to real transcripts
alternativeExplanation: The table may have been partially updated after CR-017 was later executed, leaving stale prose behind; CR-016 and CR-018 remain unsupported either way.
downgradeTrigger: Make aggregate, per-scenario, decision, verification, and evidence-path claims internally consistent, then prove every PASS has a durable transcript or mark unsupported scenarios SKIP.
recommendation: Reconstruct the actual CR-016/017/018 execution state and update implementation-summary.md accordingly.

title: The new 093 playbooks are not reachable from their owning skill files
claim: Packet 093 produced sk-code-review and sk-git manual_testing_playbook packages, but neither owning SKILL.md exposes the package from its resource routing or related-resources sections.
counterevidenceSought: Any router path that includes manual_testing_playbook
Another canonical entrypoint referenced from the owning skill
alternativeExplanation: The playbooks may be intended for direct filesystem discovery only, but that contradicts the prompt's reachability requirement and hides the packages from normal skill readers.
downgradeTrigger: Link both playbook roots from their owning SKILL.md files and, if desired, add routing text that names manual-testing use cases as on-demand resources.
recommendation: Add explicit manual testing playbook links to sk-code-review and sk-git SKILL.md.
